   207	static bool atombios_detect_v2(struct atom_context *ctx)

   212		uint8_t frev = get_u8(ctx->bios, ctx->cmd_table + 2);

   179	
   180	static uint32_t dcn10_read(void *mmio, uint32_t reg)
   181	{
   182		return read32((uint8_t *)mmio + DCN10_REG2(reg));
   183	}
   184	
   185	static void dcn10_write(void *mmio, uint32_t reg, uint32_t value)
   186	{
   187		write32((uint8_t *)mmio + DCN10_REG2(reg), value);
   188	#ifdef DCN10_TEST_TRACE
   189		dcn10_test_mmio_write(reg, value);
   190	#endif
   191	}
   192	
   193	static void dcn10_update(void *mmio, uint32_t reg, uint32_t mask, uint32_t value)
   194	{
   195		uint32_t old = dcn10_read(mmio, reg);
   196	
   197		dcn10_write(mmio, reg, (old & ~mask) | (value & mask));
   198	}
   199	
   200	static int dcn10_wait(void *mmio, uint32_t reg, uint32_t mask,
   201			      uint32_t value, unsigned int timeout_us)
   202	{
   203		while (timeout_us--) {
   204			if ((dcn10_read(mmio, reg) & mask) == value)
   205				return 0;
   206			udelay(1);
   207		}
   208		return -1;
   209	}

   211	void dcn10_disable_vga(void *mmio_base)
   212	{
   213		uint32_t vga_mode;
   214	
   215		vga_mode = read32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D1VGA_CONTROL)) |
   216			read32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D2VGA_CONTROL)) |
   217			read32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D3VGA_CONTROL)) |
   218			read32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D4VGA_CONTROL));
   219		if (!(vga_mode & 1))
   220			return;
   221	
   222		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D1VGA_CONTROL), 0);
   223		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D2VGA_CONTROL), 0);
   224		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D3VGA_CONTROL), 0);
   225		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D4VGA_CONTROL), 0);
   226		vga_mode = read32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(VGA_TEST_CONTROL));
   227		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(VGA_TEST_CONTROL),
   228			vga_mode | 0x101);
   229	}

   231	static void dcn10_reset_mpc(void *mmio, unsigned int pipe_count)
   232	{
   233		unsigned int i;
   234	
   235		for (i = 0; i < pipe_count; i++) {
   236			uint32_t base = MPCC0_TOP_SEL + i * MPCC_STRIDE;
   237	
   238			dcn10_update(mmio, base, 0xf, 0xf);
   239			dcn10_update(mmio, base + 1, 0xf, 0xf);
   240			dcn10_update(mmio, base + 2, 0xf, 0xf);
   241			dcn10_update(mmio, base + 3, 0x3, 0);
   242			dcn10_update(mmio, base + 5, 0xf, 0xf);
   243			dcn10_update(mmio, MPC_OUT0_MUX + i, 0xf, 0xf);
   244		}
   245	}
   246	
   247	int dcn10_cold_init(void *mmio_base, uint8_t pipe_count)
   248	{
   249		if (!mmio_base || !pipe_count || pipe_count > 4)
   250			return -1;
   251	
   252		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(DCCG_GATE_DISABLE_CNTL), 0);
   253		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(DCCG_GATE_DISABLE_CNTL2), 0);
   254		dcn10_update(mmio_base, DCFCLK_CNTL, 1U << 31, 0);
   255		dcn10_reset_mpc(mmio_base, pipe_count);
   256		dcn10_update(mmio_base, DCHUBP_CNTL,
   257			     HUBP_BLANK_EN | HUBP_DISABLE | HUBP_TTU_DISABLE,
   258			     HUBP_BLANK_EN | HUBP_DISABLE | HUBP_TTU_DISABLE);
   259		return 0;
   260	}

   262	static int dcn10_power_on_plane(void *mmio)
   263	{
   264		dcn10_update(mmio, DC_IP_REQUEST_CNTL, 1, 1);
   265		dcn10_update(mmio, DOMAIN1_PG_CONFIG, 1U << 8, 0);
   266		if (dcn10_wait(mmio, DOMAIN1_PG_STATUS, 3U << 30, 0, 1000))
   267			goto fail;
   268		dcn10_update(mmio, DOMAIN0_PG_CONFIG, 1U << 8, 0);
   269		if (dcn10_wait(mmio, DOMAIN0_PG_STATUS, 3U << 30, 0, 1000))
   270			goto fail;
   271		dcn10_update(mmio, DC_IP_REQUEST_CNTL, 1, 0);
   272		return 0;
   273	fail:
   274		dcn10_update(mmio, DC_IP_REQUEST_CNTL, 1, 0);
   275		return -1;
   276	}
   277	
   278	static int dcn10_lock(void *mmio, int lock)
   279	{
   280		dcn10_update(mmio, OTG_GLOBAL_CONTROL0, 0x0e000000, 0);
   281		dcn10_update(mmio, OTG_MASTER_UPDATE_LOCK, 1, lock ? 1 : 0);
   282		return dcn10_wait(mmio, OTG_MASTER_UPDATE_LOCK, 1U << 8,
   283				  lock ? 1U << 8 : 0, 1000);
   284	}
   285	
   286	int dcn10_program_stream(void *mmio, const struct dcn10_timing *timing)
   287	{
   288		struct dcn10_plane_regs calc;
   289		uint32_t h_total, v_total, h_blank_start, h_blank_end;
   290		uint32_t v_blank_start, v_blank_end, frame;
   291	
   292		if (dcn10_calculate_plane(timing,
   293					   (timing->h_active + 63U) & ~63U, &calc))
   294			return -1;
   295		h_total = timing->h_active + timing->h_blank;
   296		v_total = timing->v_active + timing->v_blank;
   297		h_blank_start = h_total - timing->h_front_porch;
   298		h_blank_end = h_blank_start - timing->h_active;
   299		v_blank_start = v_total - timing->v_front_porch;
   300		v_blank_end = v_blank_start - timing->v_active;
   301	
   302		/* optc1_enable_optc_clock() and tg_init(), still transmitting blank data. */
   303		dcn10_update(mmio, OPTC_INPUT_CLOCK_CONTROL, 0x3, 0x3);
   304		if (dcn10_wait(mmio, OPTC_INPUT_CLOCK_CONTROL, 1U << 2, 1U << 2, 1000))
   305			return -1;
   306		dcn10_update(mmio, OTG_CLOCK_CONTROL, 0x3, 0x3);
   307		if (dcn10_wait(mmio, OTG_CLOCK_CONTROL, 1U << 8, 1U << 8, 1000))
   308			return -1;
   309		dcn10_update(mmio, OTG_BLANK_CONTROL, 0x00010100, 0x00000100);
   310		dcn10_update(mmio, VTG_CONTROL, 0x80000000, 0);
   311		dcn10_update(mmio, OTG_DOUBLE_BUFFER_CONTROL, 0x00010100, 0x00000100);
   312	
   313		dcn10_write(mmio, OTG_H_TOTAL, h_total - 1);
   314		dcn10_write(mmio, OTG_H_SYNC_A, (uint32_t)timing->h_sync_width << 16);
   315		dcn10_write(mmio, OTG_H_BLANK_START_END,
   316			    h_blank_start | (h_blank_end << 16));
   317		dcn10_update(mmio, OTG_H_SYNC_A_CNTL, 1,
   318			     timing->hsync_positive ? 0 : 1);
   319		dcn10_write(mmio, OTG_H_TIMING_CNTL, 0);
   320		dcn10_write(mmio, OTG_V_TOTAL, v_total - 1);

    39	 *   BAR 0 (or 2 on some): framebuffer aperture (VRAM)
    40	 *   BAR 2 (or 5 on some): MMIO register space
    41	 * For discrete GPUs, the MMIO BAR is typically PCI_BASE_ADDRESS_2 or
    42	 * PCI_BASE_ADDRESS_5. We try BAR5 first (common for GCN+), then BAR2.
    43	 */
    44	#define AMD_GPU_MMIO_BAR	PCI_BASE_ADDRESS_5

    55	/*
    56	 * ATOM command and data table indices.
    57	 *
    58	 * The master command/data table layout differs between atombios.h (v1,
    59	 * pre-Vega) and atomfirmware.h (v2, Vega+). The ATOM bytecode interpreter
    60	 * is format-agnostic, but command table indices differ because the v2
    61	 * master table has a different field order.

    63	 * We detect the format at runtime from the master table header revision
    64	 * and store the resolved indices in an atombios_cmd_table_idx struct.
    65	 */

   202	/*
   203	 * Detect whether the VBIOS uses v1 (atombios.h) or v2 (atomfirmware.h)
   204	 * master table format by reading the format_revision of the master
   205	 * command table header.
   206	 */
   207	static bool atombios_detect_v2(struct atom_context *ctx)
   208	{
   209		/* The master command table starts with a 4-byte common header.
   210		 * format_revision is at byte offset 2. v1 has revision 1.x,
   211		 * v2 has revision 2.x. */
   212		uint8_t frev = get_u8(ctx->bios, ctx->cmd_table + 2);
   213		return frev >= 2;
   214	}

   180	static uint32_t dcn10_read(void *mmio, uint32_t reg)
   181	{
   182		return read32((uint8_t *)mmio + DCN10_REG2(reg));
   183	}
   184	
   185	static void dcn10_write(void *mmio, uint32_t reg, uint32_t value)
   186	{
   187		write32((uint8_t *)mmio + DCN10_REG2(reg), value);
   188	#ifdef DCN10_TEST_TRACE
   189		dcn10_test_mmio_write(reg, value);
   190	#endif
   191	}
   192	
   193	static void dcn10_update(void *mmio, uint32_t reg, uint32_t mask, uint32_t value)
   194	{
   195		uint32_t old = dcn10_read(mmio, reg);
   196	
   197		dcn10_write(mmio, reg, (old & ~mask) | (value & mask));
   198	}
   199	
   200	static int dcn10_wait(void *mmio, uint32_t reg, uint32_t mask,
   201			      uint32_t value, unsigned int timeout_us)
   202	{
   203		while (timeout_us--) {
   204			if ((dcn10_read(mmio, reg) & mask) == value)
   205				return 0;
   206			udelay(1);
   207		}
   208		return -1;
   209	}

   211	void dcn10_disable_vga(void *mmio_base)
   212	{
   213		uint32_t vga_mode;
   214	
   215		vga_mode = read32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D1VGA_CONTROL)) |
   216			read32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D2VGA_CONTROL)) |
   217			read32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D3VGA_CONTROL)) |
   218			read32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D4VGA_CONTROL));
   219		if (!(vga_mode & 1))
   220			return;
   221	
   222		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D1VGA_CONTROL), 0);
   223		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D2VGA_CONTROL), 0);
   224		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D3VGA_CONTROL), 0);
   225		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(D4VGA_CONTROL), 0);
   226		vga_mode = read32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(VGA_TEST_CONTROL));
   227		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(VGA_TEST_CONTROL),
   228			vga_mode | 0x101);
   229	}

   231	static void dcn10_reset_mpc(void *mmio, unsigned int pipe_count)
   232	{
   233		unsigned int i;
   234	
   235		for (i = 0; i < pipe_count; i++) {
   236			uint32_t base = MPCC0_TOP_SEL + i * MPCC_STRIDE;
   237	
   238			dcn10_update(mmio, base, 0xf, 0xf);
   239			dcn10_update(mmio, base + 1, 0xf, 0xf);
   240			dcn10_update(mmio, base + 2, 0xf, 0xf);
   241			dcn10_update(mmio, base + 3, 0x3, 0);
   242			dcn10_update(mmio, base + 5, 0xf, 0xf);
   243			dcn10_update(mmio, MPC_OUT0_MUX + i, 0xf, 0xf);
   244		}
   245	}
   246	
   247	int dcn10_cold_init(void *mmio_base, uint8_t pipe_count)
   248	{
   249		if (!mmio_base || !pipe_count || pipe_count > 4)
   250			return -1;
   251	
   252		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(DCCG_GATE_DISABLE_CNTL), 0);
   253		write32((uint8_t *)mmio_base + DCN10_REG1(DCCG_GATE_DISABLE_CNTL2), 0);
   254		dcn10_update(mmio_base, DCFCLK_CNTL, 1U << 31, 0);
   255		dcn10_reset_mpc(mmio_base, pipe_count);
   256		dcn10_update(mmio_base, DCHUBP_CNTL,
   257			     HUBP_BLANK_EN | HUBP_DISABLE | HUBP_TTU_DISABLE,
   258			     HUBP_BLANK_EN | HUBP_DISABLE | HUBP_TTU_DISABLE);
   259		return 0;
   260	}

   262	static int dcn10_power_on_plane(void *mmio)
   263	{
   264		dcn10_update(mmio, DC_IP_REQUEST_CNTL, 1, 1);

   268		dcn10_update(mmio, DOMAIN0_PG_CONFIG, 1U << 8, 0);
   269		if (dcn10_wait(mmio, DOMAIN0_PG_STATUS, 3U << 30, 0, 1000))
   270			goto fail;
   271		dcn10_update(mmio, DC_IP_REQUEST_CNTL, 1, 0);

   273	fail:
   274		dcn10_update(mmio, DC_IP_REQUEST_CNTL, 1, 0);
   275		return -1;
   276	}

   278	static int dcn10_lock(void *mmio, int lock)
   279	{

   281		dcn10_update(mmio, OTG_MASTER_UPDATE_LOCK, 1, lock ? 1 : 0);

   283				  lock ? 1U << 8 : 0, 1000);

   290		uint32_t v_blank_start, v_blank_end, frame;

   295		h_total = timing->h_active + timing->h_blank;
   296		v_total = timing->v_active + timing->v_blank;
   297		h_blank_start = h_total - timing->h_front_porch;
   298		h_blank_end = h_blank_start - timing->h_active;
   299		v_blank_start = v_total - timing->v_front_porch;
   300		v_blank_end = v_blank_start - timing->v_active;

   308			return -1;
   309		dcn10_update(mmio, OTG_BLANK_CONTROL, 0x00010100, 0x00000100);
   310		dcn10_update(mmio, VTG_CONTROL, 0x80000000, 0);
   311		dcn10_update(mmio, OTG_DOUBLE_BUFFER_CONTROL, 0x00010100, 0x00000100);
   312	
   313		dcn10_write(mmio, OTG_H_TOTAL, h_total - 1);
   314		dcn10_write(mmio, OTG_H_SYNC_A, (uint32_t)timing->h_sync_width << 16);
   315		dcn10_write(mmio, OTG_H_BLANK_START_END,

# SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0-only

# Place the build output in one of two places depending on COV, so that code
# built with code coverage never mixes with code built without code coverage.
ifeq ($(COV),1)
testobj := $(obj)/coverage
else
testobj := $(obj)/tests
endif

objutil := $(testobj)/util

include $(top)/tests/Makefile.common

# Enable GDB debug build if requested
GDB_DEBUG ?= 0
ifneq ($(GDB_DEBUG),0)
TEST_CFLAGS += -g -Og
endif

# Enable code coverage if requested
ifeq ($(COV),1)
TEST_CFLAGS += --coverage
TEST_LDFLAGS += --coverage
endif

# Use system cmoka in default, or build from 3rdparty source code if requested
USE_SYSTEM_CMOCKA ?= 1
ifeq ($(USE_SYSTEM_CMOCKA),1)
ifeq ($(shell $(HOSTPKG_CONFIG) --exists cmocka || echo 1),1)
$(warning No system cmocka, build from 3rdparty instead...)
USE_SYSTEM_CMOCKA=0
endif
endif

stages := decompressor bootblock romstage smm verstage
stages += ramstage rmodule postcar libagesa

alltests :=
subdirs := tests/arch tests/acpi tests/commonlib tests/console tests/cpu
subdirs += tests/device tests/drivers tests/ec tests/lib
subdirs += tests/mainboard tests/northbridge tests/security tests/soc
subdirs += tests/southbridge tests/superio tests/vendorcode

define tests-handler
alltests += $(1)$(2)
$(foreach attribute,$(attributes),
	$(eval $(1)$(2)-$(attribute) += $($(2)-$(attribute))))
$(foreach attribute,$(attributes),
	$(eval $(2)-$(attribute) := ))

# Sanity check for stage attribute value
$(eval $(1)$(2)-stage := $(if $($(1)$(2)-stage),$($(1)$(2)-stage),ramstage))
$(if $(findstring $($(1)$(2)-stage), $(stages)),,
	$(error Wrong $(1)$(2)-stage value $($(1)$(2)-stage). \
		Check your $(dir $(1)$(2))Makefile.mk))
endef

$(call add-special-class, tests)
$(call evaluate_subdirs)

$(foreach test, $(alltests), \
	$(eval $(test)-srcobjs := $(addprefix $(testobj)/$(test)/, \
		$(patsubst %.c,%.o,$(filter src/%,$($(test)-srcs))))) \
	$(eval $(test)-sysobjs := $(addprefix $(testobj)/$(test)/, \
		$(patsubst %.c,%.o,$($(test)-syssrcs)))) \
	$(eval $(test)-objs := $(addprefix $(testobj)/$(test)/, \
		$(patsubst %.c,%.o,$($(test)-srcs)))))
$(foreach test, $(alltests), \
	$(eval $(test)-bin := $(testobj)/$(test)/run))
$(foreach test, $(alltests), \
	$(eval $(call TEST_CC_template,$(test))))

$(foreach test, $(alltests), \
	$(eval all-test-objs += $($(test)-objs)))
$(foreach test, $(alltests), \
	$(eval test-bins += $($(test)-bin)))

DEPENDENCIES += $(addsuffix .d,$(basename $(all-test-objs)))
-include $(DEPENDENCIES)

.PHONY: $(alltests) $(addprefix clean-,$(alltests)) $(addprefix try-,$(alltests))
.PHONY: $(addprefix build-,$(alltests)) $(addprefix run-,$(alltests))
.PHONY: unit-tests build-unit-tests run-unit-tests clean-unit-tests
.PHONY: junit.xml-unit-tests clean-junit.xml-unit-tests

# %g in CMOCKA_XML_FILE will be replaced with "__TEST_NAME__(<test-group-name>)"
# by macro cb_run_group_tests(), which should be used for running tests.
# __TEST_NAME__ contains test name including path e.g. tests_lib_rtc-test
ifeq ($(JUNIT_OUTPUT),y)
$(addprefix run-,$(alltests)): export CMOCKA_MESSAGE_OUTPUT=xml
$(addprefix run-,$(alltests)): export CMOCKA_XML_FILE=$(testobj)/junit-%g.xml
endif

$(addprefix run-,$(alltests)): run-%: $$(%-bin)
	rm -f $(testobj)/junit-$(subst /,_,$(patsubst $(testobj)/%/,%,$(dir $^)))\(*\).xml
	rm -f $(testobj)/$(subst /,_,$^).failed
	-$^ || echo failed > $(testobj)/$(subst /,_,$^).failed

$(addprefix build-,$(alltests)): build-%: $$(%-bin)

## Review

- Correct: The intended ownership split is mostly visible in the code: AtomFirmware/ATOM tables handle ASIC_Init, display-pipe power-gating, PLL, DIG encoder, transmitter/PHY, AUX, panel power and backlight (`src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4509`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4555`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4598`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4620`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4632`), while direct DCN code owns stream timing and the HUBP/DPP/MPC/OPP plane path (`src/drivers/amd/atombios/dcn10.c:286`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/dcn10.c:435`). This broadly matches the Linux DCN split of BIOS services for link/PHY and DC programming for timing/planes.
- Correct: The happy-path sequence for the Picasso-only branch is coherent: v2 ASIC_Init, DCN pipe/golden init, panel power, DisplayObjectInfo parse/AUX EDID, Atom link preparation, DCN stream, Atom link training, DCN scanout, backlight, framebuffer registration (`src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4790`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4795`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4803`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4810`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4865`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4874`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4881`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4887`). The framebuffer is registered only after scanout/backlight success.
- Correct: Unit coverage exists for the DCN1 plane math/commit ordering/fail-closed local-FB check and AtomFirmware v2 table bounds parsing (`tests/drivers/dcn10-test.c:137`, `tests/drivers/dcn10-test.c:189`, `tests/drivers/atombios-v2-test.c:80`, `tests/drivers/atombios-v2-test.c:113`). The proper coreboot test targets pass.

- Blocker: `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4327`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4329`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4795`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4846` - DCN1 orchestration is gated to device `0x15d8` only, even though the v2 display-IP classifier says both `0x15dd` and `0x15d8` are DCN 1.0/1.01. On `0x15dd`, the code can run AtomFirmware v2 ASIC_Init and AUX/EDID logging, then return without `dcn10_disable_vga()`, DCN pipe init, stream/plane programming, backlight, or framebuffer registration. Use the display-IP enum (or include all DCN10 IDs) for the native DCN1 path instead of the single PCI ID.
- Blocker: `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4530`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4540`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4803`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4819`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4846`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4591`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4881` - eDP ownership is not actually derived from DisplayObjectInfo. `atomfirmware_power_edp()` powers a hard-coded eDP path on HPD/DIG/PHY 0 before the v2 display paths are parsed, and the later EDID loop treats the first DPCD-responsive path as an eDP panel, hard-coding `CONNECTOR_OBJECT_ID_eDP` and issuing LCD backlight-on. This can power/train/backlight the wrong PHY or apply eDP panel sequencing to an external DP connector. Parse/select the actual eDP/LVDS_eDP path first and use its AUX/HPD/PHY/DIG; skip LCD_BLON and panel power for external DP.
- Blocker: `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4803`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4806`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4865`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4885`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/dcn10.c:337`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/dcn10.c:344`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/dcn10.c:555`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/dcn10.c:560` - failure rollback is incomplete after irreversible display steps. Once panel power, OTG master enable, transmitter enable/link training, or HUBP unblank has happened, later failures return directly without an ordered rollback. One concrete DCN case: after writing the surface address and clearing HUBP blank/disable bits, `dcn10_program_scanout()` returns immediately if unlock fails, without reblanking or disabling the plane. Add a single rollback path for the v2 sequence: backlight off if enabled, DP video/transmitter disable, panel power off for eDP, OTG blank/disable, HUBP blank/disable, unlock if locked, and no framebuffer registration.
- Note: `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4704`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4716`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/dcn10.c:445`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/dcn10.c:447`, `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4874`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4879` - for expected v2 devices, a missing/zero FB BAR is only a warning, but DCN scanout rejects null CPU/GPU addresses after the panel/link/stream are already up. Validate `fb_bar`, `fb_size`, and `v2_fw.mc_base` before panel power/link training, or route the error through the rollback path above.
- Note: `src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4790`-`src/drivers/amd/atombios/atombios_driver.c:4802` - the v2/DCN1 path is cold-only: it always runs ASIC_Init and DCN cold init and has no warm-state branch to preserve/register an already-active GOP/simplefb handoff or to do an ordered disable before reset. If warm takeover is in scope, add a small state check before destructive init; otherwise document that this path is cold native init only.

Plan/progress note: `/home/arthur/src/coreboot/plan.md` and `/home/arthur/src/coreboot/progress.md` were requested but are not present in this checkout, so this audit is based on the current working tree files and tests.
